g.data-narration: Welcome to this lecture on Different Types of Queues. We'll explore how variations solve real-world problems.
g.text: Understanding Queue Variations for Efficient Software Design

g.data-narration: The Simple Queue follows FIFO but has a critical flaw: once full, you can't add more even if front spaces are empty.
g.text: Standard queue follows FIFO: Insert at Rear, Delete at Front
But wait! Index 0-3 are empty after dequeues
Yet we cannot add new elements - this is the limitation!

g.data-narration: To solve these limitations, computer scientists developed four main queue variations. Let's explore each one.
g.text: Four Types of Queues We'll Explore

g.data-narration: The Simple Queue is the foundation - insertion at rear, deletion at front, but suffers from inefficient memory usage.
g.text: Basic FIFO structure - insertion at rear, deletion at front
Rear reaches MAX but front has empty spaces!

g.data-narration: Circular Queue solves this by connecting the end of the array back to the beginning using modulo arithmetic.
g.text: Array is treated as circular - rear wraps to beginning when it reaches the end

g.data-narration: Watch how elements wrap around! After reaching index 4, the next element goes to index 0.
Priority Queue breaks FIFO - elements with higher priority get processed first regardless of arrival order.
g.text: Elements have priority; highest priority dequeues first

g.data-narration: Priority queues are commonly implemented using Heaps - a tree structure that gives O(log n) performance.
g.text: Often implemented using Heap data structure for efficiency

g.data-narration: Deque (double-ended queue) allows insertion and deletion from both front and rear ends - maximum flexibility.
g.text: Insertion and deletion from BOTH ends - front and rear

g.data-narration: There are two special types: Input-restricted allows insertion only at rear, Output-restricted allows deletion only at front.
g.text: ✏️ Insert: Rear only | 🗑️ Delete: Front & Rear
✏️ Insert: Front & Rear | 🗑️ Delete: Front only
📱 Applications: Browser history, Undo/Redo, Palindrome checker

g.data-narration: Let's compare all queue types to understand their differences and choose the right one for each scenario.
Each queue type has specific real-world applications. Understanding these helps choose the right one for your problem.
